name: Kensington Capital Acquisition Corp. VI
business: Kensington Capital Acquisition Corp. VI is a blank check company incorporated as a Cayman Islands exempted company for the purpose of effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization or similar business combination with one or more businesses or entities. The company has not selected any potential business combination target and has not had any substantive discussions with any potential business combination target. The company intends to focus on opportunities in the global automotive and automotive-related sector, as well as other high-growth sectors including defense, energy and artificial intelligence.

exposures.sentence: We will seek to capitalize on the significant experience and vast network of our management team to complete our initial business combination.

We would seek to capitalize on automotive mega trends including next-gen battery technology, electric powertrains, automotive software, autonomous driving hardware and software, and robotics, eVTOLs and drones.

We would seek to capitalize on automotive mega trends including next-gen battery technology, electric powertrains, automotive software, autonomous driving hardware and software, and robotics, eVTOLs and drones.

We currently intend to focus on opportunities that capitalize on the expertise and ability of our management team, particularly our executive officers, to identify, acquire and operate a business in the global automotive and automotive-related sector (our Focus Industries ), as well as other high-growth sectors, including defense, energy and artificial intelligence ( AI ).

catalysts.event: If we have not completed our initial business combination within 24 months from the closing of this offering or such earlier liquidation date as our board of directors may approve, or during any Extension Period, subject to applicable law, we will redeem our public shares.
catalysts.sentence: If we have not completed our initial business combination within 24 months from the closing of this offering or such earlier liquidation date as our board of directors may approve, or during any Extension Period, subject to applicable law, we will be required to redeem our public shares.

tamClaims.claim: The automotive industry represents a total addressable market of more than $5.5 trillion and 10,000+ companies across three main segments.
tamClaims.sentence: The automotive industry represents a total addressable market of more than $5.5 trillion and 10,000+ companies across three main segments: the automotive aftermarket, emerging technologies and Original Equipment Manufacturer ( OEM ) and Original Equipment ( OE ) suppliers.

tamClaims.claim: Global battery demand is projected to grow significantly across all scenarios, with estimates ranging from approximately 2,000 GWh in 2025 to between 3,000 GWh and 5,000 GWh by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to McKinsey & Company, global battery demand is projected to grow significantly across all scenarios, with estimates ranging from approximately 2,000 GWh in 2025 to between 3,000 GWh and 5,000 GWh by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: The global automotive powertrain systems market is projected to expand from approximately $470 billion in 2025 to $690 billion by 2030, reflecting a robust 7.9% compound annual growth rate (CAGR).
tamClaims.sentence: The global automotive powertrain systems market is projected to expand from approximately $470 billion in 2025 to $690 billion by 2030, reflecting a robust 7.9% compound annual growth rate (CAGR), with hybrid technologies also playing a critical role in the transition toward cleaner mobility.

tamClaims.claim: The market for automotive software is projected to expand from approximately $352 billion in 2025 to $462 billion by 2030, with $83 billion in software development revenue potential by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to McKinsey & Company, the market is projected to expand from approximately $352 billion in 2025 to $462 billion by 2030, with $83 billion in software development revenue potential by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: McKinsey & Company estimates the market potential for advanced driver-assistance features in passenger vehicles to reach approximately $300 to $400 billion by 2035.
tamClaims.sentence: McKinsey & Company estimates the market potential for advanced driver-assistance features in passenger vehicles to reach approximately $300 to $400 billion by 2035.

tamClaims.claim: The global drone market is expected to grow from approximately $84 billion in 2025 to $164 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: The global drone market is expected to grow from approximately $84 billion in 2025 to $164 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: The mobile robotics market is projected to expand from $29 billion to $74 billion over the same period.
tamClaims.sentence: The mobile robotics market is projected to expand from $29 billion to $74 billion over the same period.

tamClaims.claim: Precedence Research estimates the eVTOL (electric vertical takeoff and landing) market will reach approximately $170 billion by 2034.
tamClaims.sentence: Additionally, Precedence Research estimates the eVTOL (electric vertical takeoff and landing) market will reach approximately $170 billion by 2034, driven by urban air mobility and advancements in sustainable aerial travel.
